0.title: рҹӨ– Enterprise Multi-Agent Architecture
0.content: вңҚпёҸ Author: kaul-vineet В· Principal PM Architect В· vineetkaul@microsoft.com . Design Requirements/Priorities . | рҹ§© No-code over custom features | рҹ“Ҳ Scale over custom development | рҹҸӯ 100+ agents in 6вҖ“12 months | рҹ”ө Reduced complexity at every layer | вҳҒпёҸ Managed services over custom infrastructure | рҹҺҜ Simplest effective approach wins | . Informed by: Microsoft CAF В· Azure Architecture Center В· DeepLearning.AI В· HuggingFace Research В· Gartner В· MAST В· smolagents .

1.title: рҹ§  Key Architectural Principles
1.content: Consolidated from DeepLearning.AI, HuggingFace / MAST, smolagents, Microsoft CAF, Azure Architecture Center, and Azure Well-Architected Framework: . | # | Principle | Source | Applied To This Architecture | . | 1пёҸвғЈ | Minimise LLM calls | HuggingFace / smolagents | Default to deterministic CS Topics. Every added LLM call is a new error vector. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ | Default to Flows, escalate to Crews | DeepLearning.AI | CS Topics + PA flows for 80%. Foundry with planning for 20% open-ended tasks. | . | 3пёҸвғЈ | Clarification before action | MAST (FM-2.2) | Ambiguity causes 50-point accuracy drop. Build disambiguation topics before every agentic branch. | . | 4пёҸвғЈ | External verification вҖ” never self-check | MAST (FM-3.3) | Every write action followed by deterministic read-back. Never ask the generating agent to confirm its own output. | . | 5пёҸвғЈ | Externalize termination logic | MAST (FM-1.5, FM-3.1) | Stopping conditions in Foundry orchestration code вҖ” max step count, loop detector. Never in LLM prompt. | . | 6пёҸвғЈ | Tool argument quality is the #1 failure cause | HuggingFace (>50% of failures) | Every connector needs explicit argument formats with examples, structured error responses, RFC3339 datetime formats. | . | 7пёҸвғЈ | Consolidate tools | HuggingFace / smolagents | Merge tools where possible вҖ” one call that does two things is strictly better than two sequential calls. | . | 8пёҸвғЈ | Multi-agent is a reliability tax | HuggingFace / AssetOpsBench | Every agent boundary costs accuracy. Add agent boundaries only at genuine compliance/security seams. | . | 9пёҸвғЈ | Context hygiene at scale | HuggingFace / Forge RL | Compaction step every N turns. Scope context passed between agents to what is strictly necessary. | . | рҹ”ҹ | Sensitive state in side channel | HuggingFace / Neuro SAN | Credentials, PII, session IDs never in LLM prompt. Use Key Vault references resolved at tool execution time. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ1пёҸвғЈ | Tool accuracy > 90% as deployment gate | HuggingFace / AssetOpsBench | Below 90% tool accuracy, do not promote to production. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ2пёҸвғЈ | Human-in-the-loop for irreversible actions | DeepLearning.AI В· Microsoft CAF | PA approval flows as deterministic gates before write-backs to financial systems, CRM, or HR records. Two-phase: propose вҶ’ dry-run вҶ’ execute. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ3пёҸвғЈ | Use lowest complexity that reliably works | Azure Architecture Center | Evaluate direct model call вҶ’ single agent вҶ’ multi-agent in that order. Do not add agent layers without a demonstrated failure reason. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ4пёҸвғЈ | Justify every agent boundary explicitly | Azure Architecture Center | Only split work across agents when single-agent fails due to prompt complexity, tool overload, or security boundary. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ5пёҸвғЈ | Single responsibility per agent | Azure Architecture Center В· Microsoft CAF | Each agent has a narrowly defined domain. Monitor and prevent agent overlap вҖ” redundant agents degrade routing accuracy. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ6пёҸвғЈ | Right-size the model per agent role | Azure Architecture Center | Not every agent needs the most capable model. Classification, extraction, and formatting agents can use smaller, cheaper models. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ7пёҸвғЈ | Validate output before passing downstream | Azure Architecture Center | Low-confidence or malformed outputs must not propagate. Orchestrator checks quality and retries, requests clarification, or halts. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ8пёҸвғЈ | Surface errors explicitly вҖ” never swallow failures | Azure Architecture Center | Include structured error responses on every tool. Downstream agents and orchestrators must be able to respond to failures. | . | 1пёҸвғЈ9пёҸвғЈ | Idempotency for all writes | Microsoft Tech Community | Every write operation must be safe to repeat. Retries must not duplicate side effects. Assign an idempotency key to every state-changing tool call. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ0пёҸвғЈ | Kill switch вҖ” disable tools without redeployment | Microsoft CAF | Define in advance how to stop tool execution, downgrade to read-only, isolate a capability, and communicate incidents fleet-wide. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ1пёҸвғЈ | Security trimming in every agent, not just the edge | Azure Architecture Center | Agents have broad knowledge access but must not return data inaccessible to the user. Trimming must be enforced at each agent, not only at the gateway. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ2пёҸвғЈ | Content safety at every layer | Azure Architecture Center | Apply guardrails at user input, tool calls, tool responses, and final output. Intermediate agents can introduce or propagate harmful content. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ3пёҸвғЈ | Scope agent authority to operations, not tools | Microsoft Tech Community | Not вҖңallow JiraвҖқ but вҖңcreate ticket only.вҖқ Apply rate limits and blast radius caps per agent per run. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ4пёҸвғЈ | Instrument all handoffs, not just final output | Azure Architecture Center В· Microsoft Azure Blog | Distributed agent orchestrations require tracing at every agent boundary and tool call, not only the terminal response. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ5пёҸвғЈ | Automated evaluation in CI/CD | Microsoft Azure Blog | Every code or prompt change triggers automated quality and safety evaluation before release. Use LLM-as-Judge вҖ” exact-match assertions do not work for nondeterministic outputs. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ6пёҸвғЈ | Evaluate continuously in production | Microsoft Azure Blog | Evaluate intent resolution, tool selection, task adherence, and response completeness throughout the agent lifecycle вҖ” not only at release. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ7пёҸвғЈ | Maintain agent inventory вҖ” shadow agents are a risk | Microsoft CAF | Untracked deployments pose security and cost risks. Every agent must be registered, owned, and versioned. | . | 2пёҸвғЈ8пёҸвғЈ | Externalize and version-control prompts | Azure Well-Architected Framework | Treat prompts as versioned configuration, not inline code. Implement controlled rollout for prompt changes. | . рҹ“ҡ References . | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” Agentic Design Patterns | MAST Failure Taxonomy | AssetOpsBench | HuggingFace вҖ” Design Patterns for Agentic Workflows | smolagents | OpenEnv вҖ” Tool Argument Failures | Neuro SAN вҖ” Side Channel Pattern | Agent Leaderboard вҖ” Tool Quality Benchmarks | Azure Architecture Center вҖ” AI Agent Orchestration Patterns | Microsoft CAF вҖ” Governance and Security for AI Agents | Azure Well-Architected Framework вҖ” Application Design for AI Workloads | Microsoft Copilot Studio вҖ” Multi-Agent Patterns | Microsoft Azure Blog вҖ” Agent Observability Best Practices | Microsoft Tech Community вҖ” AI Production Architecture | Microsoft for Developers вҖ” Designing Multi-Agent Intelligence | .

2.title: рҹ“җ Recommendation Criteria
2.content: Every recommendation is evaluated against five criteria: . | В  | Criterion | Meaning | . | вҡЎ | High Speed of Development | First agent ships in days, not weeks | . | рҹ§© | No-Code Over Custom Features | Platform-native features even at 80% coverage, rather than custom code for 100% | . | рҹ“Ҳ | Scale Over Custom Dev | Works for agent #1 and agent #100 without re-architecting | . | рҹҸӯ | 100+ Agents in 6вҖ“12 Months | Agent factory вҖ” templates, ALM pipelines, domain reuse | . | рҹ”ө | Reduced Complexity | Fewest moving parts, fewest technologies, fewest custom servers | . вҡҷпёҸ Flows vs. Crews . | В  | рҹ”ө Flow (Deterministic) | рҹҹ  Crew (Autonomous) | . | Nature | Sequential, explicit steps | Exploratory, emergent | . | Reliability | High | Lower вҖ” each step adds failure risk | . | Best for | Compliance, known workflows | Research, genuinely open-ended tasks | . | Microsoft mapping | CS Topics В· PA Flows В· Foundry Workflows | Foundry Agent Service with planning | . | Use ratio | ~80% of tasks | ~20% of tasks | . рҹ’Ў HuggingFace / smolagents principle: вҖңRegularize towards not using any agentic behaviour.вҖқ Reduce LLM calls to the minimum. Every added LLM call is a new error vector. рҹ§  Agentic Pattern Reliability Hierarchy . Based on Andrew Ng (DeepLearning.AI) and MAST empirical research: . RELIABILITY (most вҶ’ least mature) в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ рҹ”§ Tool Use / Deterministic Flow в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ CS Topics, PA flows, known workflows вҶ’ Default for ~80% of all tasks рҹ”Ғ Reflection / External Verification в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ Separate critic agent or deterministic check вҶ’ Add to Foundry agents for quality-sensitive outputs вҶ’ NEVER let an agent verify its own output рҹ“Ӣ Planning в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ Dynamic task decomposition вҶ’ Only for genuinely open-ended exploratory tasks вҶ’ Never for known workflows рҹӨқ Multi-Agent Collaboration в–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳв–Ҳ Each agent handoff costs ~10вҖ“15 accuracy points (68% single-agent вҶ’ 47% multi-agent) вҶ’ Use only where domain isolation is genuinely required в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ .

LEGEND рҹҹҰ Copilot Studio рҹҹ§ Azure AI Foundry рҹҹҘ Non-Microsoft (Agentforce, ServiceNowвҖҰ) . рҹ“ҡ References . | Microsoft CAF вҖ” AI Agents | Azure Architecture Center вҖ” AI Agent Orchestration Patterns | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” Agentic Design Patterns | MAST вҖ” Why Enterprise Agents Fail | smolagents вҖ” Building Good Agents | Gartner вҖ” Multiagent Systems | Microsoft Agent Factory Blog | . рҹҸҶ Recommendation . вҡЎрҹ§©рҹ“ҲрҹҸӯрҹ”ө Use Copilot Studio as gateway and domain agents with Dataverse as the operational data layer. Add Foundry only for specialist agents. This requires no custom infrastructure, deploys through Power Platform Pipelines, and lets domain teams own their agents using no-code tooling. рҹҺҜ Agent Orchestration . вң… Recommended: Gateway вҶ’ Domain Agent Hierarchy . рҹ‘Ө User в”Ӯ в–ј рҹҡӘ Gateway (1 agent вҖ” routes only, owns zero tools) в”Ӯ в”ңв”Җв”Җв–ә рҹ“Ұ HR Domain Agent вҶ’ manages вүӨ8 child/connected agents + tools в”ңв”Җв”Җв–ә рҹ“Ұ Finance Domain Agent вҶ’ manages вүӨ8 child/connected agents + tools в”ңв”Җв”Җв–ә рҹ“Ұ IT Domain Agent вҶ’ manages вүӨ8 child/connected agents + tools в””в”Җв”Җв–ә рҹ“Ұ Sales Domain Agent вҶ’ manages вүӨ8 child/connected agents + tools Rules: Each domain agent manages вүӨ 8вҖ“10 specialist agents (child or connected) Each domain agent has a bounded tool set вҖ” keep scope focused Each domain agent has its own Entra identity + team owner Each domain agent maintains a dynamic task ledger (auditable record of subtask state вҖ” not just LLM mental model) . рҹ“җ Why This Architecture Works вҖ” Research Evidence . The Gateway вҶ’ Domain hierarchy is the Orchestrator-Workers pattern, documented and validated across multiple independent research sources: . вҖңIn this workflow, a central LLM dynamically decomposes tasks, delegates them to worker agents, and synthesizes their outputs into coherent results.вҖқ вҖ” HuggingFace, Design Patterns for Agentic Workflows . вҖңBy decomposing work, developers can tailor prompts for specific objectivesвҖҰ Many teams are getting good results with this method, and ablation studies show that multiple agents give superior performance to a single agent.вҖқ вҖ” Andrew Ng, DeepLearning.AI . вҖңWhen you use multiple AI agents, you can break down complex problems into specialized units of work or knowledge. Individual agents can focus on a specific domain or capability, which reduces code and prompt complexity.вҖқ вҖ” Microsoft Azure Architecture Center . вҖңInstead of expecting one AI to be an all-knowing expert (often unrealistic вҖ” akin to getting a high-school intern when you hoped for a PhD), multi-agent orchestrators break problems into smaller pieces and assign them to specialized agents.вҖқ вҖ” HuggingFace, Neuro SAN . Four properties that make hierarchical orchestration reliable (Microsoft Azure Architecture Center): . | Property | What It Means in Practice | . | Specialization | Each domain agent has a focused prompt and bounded tool set вҖ” no competing priorities, no routing ambiguity | . | Scalability | Agents can be added or modified without redesigning the entire system вҖ” new domain = one new domain agent | . | Maintainability | Testing and debugging focused on individual agents вҖ” failures isolated, not system-wide | . | Optimization | Each domain agent can use distinct models, tools, and compute appropriate to its workload | . Key performance evidence: . | Finding | Data | Source | . | Agent loop vs. zero-shot single pass | GPT-3.5 with agent loop: 95.1% vs GPT-4 zero-shot: 67% on HumanEval | DeepLearning.AI | . | Tool accuracy as #1 differentiator | Top agents: 94% tool accuracy vs low performers: 61% вҖ” 33pp gap | AssetOpsBench (IBM) | . | Ambiguity causes accuracy collapse | Explicit intent: 90% success вҶ’ ambiguous intent: 40% success (вҲ’50pp) | OpenEnv/Turing (HuggingFace) | . | Context history loss at scale | Overloaded model: 24% of traces lose conversation history vs 0% for focused agents | MAST/ITBench (IBM) | . | Reasoning disconnect in overloaded agents | 94% of traces show reasoning-action decoupling in overloaded systems | MAST/ITBench (IBM) | . | Failure mode density | Clean agents: 2.6 failure modes/trace В· Collapsed systems: 5.3 failure modes/trace | MAST/ITBench (IBM) | . вқҢ The Star Topology Anti-Pattern (One Master Agent for All Domains) . ANTI-PATTERN HR Agent в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ Finance Agent в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө IT Agent в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Өв”Җв”Җв–ә вҡ пёҸ SINGLE SHARED ORCHESTRATOR Sales Agent в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө (handles all 100 agents) ... 95 more в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ . | вҡ пёҸ Disadvantage | What Happens in Practice | Research Backing | . | рҹ”ҙ Tool overload collapses routing quality | A single orchestrator for 100 agents accumulates hundreds of tools. LLM routing accuracy drops sharply as tool count grows вҖ” the orchestrator cannot reliably select the right agent. | вҖңAs the number of knowledge sources and tools increases, it becomes difficult to provide a predictable agent experience.вҖқ вҖ” Microsoft Azure Architecture Center | . | рҹ”ҙ Context window floods with unrelated history | All domain interactions accumulate in one shared thread. Finance queries appear in HR context. Attention dilutes across irrelevant history. Directly causes FM-1.4 (Loss of Conversation History вҖ” 24% of traces in overloaded systems). | вҖңContext windows can grow rapidly because each agent adds its own reasoning, tool results, and intermediate outputs.вҖқ вҖ” Microsoft Azure Architecture Center В· MAST FM-1.4 | . | рҹ”ҙ Routing classification fails under scope growth | A single orchestrator must classify every intent across all 100 domains from one prompt. Misclassification sends the request to the wrong domain. | вҖңIf a task is misclassified, it might be sent to the wrong agent, leading to inefficiency or incorrect results.вҖқ вҖ” HuggingFace, Design Patterns for Agentic Workflows | . | рҹ”ҙ One rate limit ceiling for all domains | All 100 agents share one model endpointвҖҷs TPM quota. A Finance spike throttles HR users simultaneously. | вҖңEvaluate how using a single MaaS endpoint can result in rate limiting when agents run concurrently.вҖқ вҖ” Microsoft Azure Architecture Center | . | рҹ”ҙ Security trimming cannot be enforced | HR and Finance queries share context and identity. Per-domain data access cannot be scoped. Over-privileged identities are the highest-impact incident category. | вҖңSecurity trimming must be implemented in every agent, not only at the gateway.вҖқ вҖ” Microsoft Azure Architecture Center В· Microsoft Tech Community | . | рҹ”ҙ Chain composition creates unreviewed capabilities | Tools approved individually compose into capabilities nobody reviewed. | вҖңRead data, create ticket, send message вҖ” all approved individually. Put together, it becomes a capability nobody reviewed.вҖқ вҖ” Microsoft Tech Community | . | рҹ”ҙ One failure = full outage for all domains | A prompt change, model degradation, or bug takes all 100 agents offline simultaneously. Failure modes cascade вҖ” overloaded systems accumulate 5.3 failure modes per failed trace vs 2.6 in focused systems. | MAST/ITBench (IBM) вҖ” Cascading Collapse | . | рҹ”ҙ ALM becomes impossible at scale | Updating any domain requires regression testing the shared orchestrator against all 100 agents. Deployment frequency drops to near zero. | Microsoft Azure Architecture Center вҖ” Maintainability principle | . | рҹ”ҙ No team ownership вҖ” governance fails | Ownership ambiguity means no team is accountable for quality or incidents. Shadow agents proliferate. | Microsoft CAF вҖ” Principle 27 (Maintain agent inventory) | . | рҹҹЎ Unpredictable and inefficient cost | Simple FAQ queries invoke the same full-context orchestrator as complex multi-step financial workflows. | Azure Architecture Center вҖ” Optimization principle | . рҹ“ҡ References . | HuggingFace вҖ” Design Patterns for Agentic Workflows | HuggingFace вҖ” IBM MAST Failure Taxonomy | HuggingFace вҖ” IBM AssetOpsBench | HuggingFace вҖ” Neuro SAN Decentralized Routing | HuggingFace вҖ” OpenEnv/Turing Tool + Routing Evaluation | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” Multi-Agent Collaboration | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” How Agents Improve LLM Performance | Azure Architecture Center вҖ” AI Agent Orchestration Patterns | Microsoft Tech Community вҖ” AI DidnвҖҷt Break Your Production | MAST Paper (arXiv) | . рҹҸҶ Recommendation . Never use a single orchestrator for all agents. Research confirms that overloaded single orchestrators accumulate cascading failures (5.3 failure modes/trace), lose conversation history (24% of traces), and suffer reasoning-action disconnect (94% of traces). One domain agent per business function. Gateway routes intent only вҖ” owns zero tools. Each domain agent manages вүӨ 8вҖ“10 specialist agents with a bounded tool set and dynamic task ledger. This architecture scales from 10 to 200 agents without redesign.

6.title: 10. вҡ пёҸ Design Considerations
6.content: Open items identified during architecture review. To be resolved before production scale-out. DC-1 В· Gateway Routing Capacity at Scale . Gap: The CS instruction limit is 8,000 characters. At 8вҖ“12 domain agents, each requiring a precise routing description plus system safety instructions, the gateway prompt approaches this ceiling. The star topology anti-pattern section correctly warns that routing accuracy collapses as scope grows вҖ” but the gateway itself faces this exact problem. Options to evaluate: . | Route by intent taxonomy using a structured CS topic with a bounded decision tree вҖ” not open-ended LLM prompt matching | Externalise routing descriptions to a Dataverse lookup table; inject only the matched description at runtime | Measure token consumption of the gateway prompt at 6, 9, and 12 domain agents before committing to a pattern | . DC-2 В· Dependent Cross-Domain Workflows . Gap: The architecture handles independent multi-domain calls correctly (leave + Salesforce: gateway calls HR domain, calls Agentforce, composes вҖ” no issue). The gap is specifically dependent cross-domain workflows: where steps have ordering constraints, shared state, compensation requirements, or duration exceeding the CS session limit (60 min / 100 turns). Example: Employee offboarding вҖ” IT access revocation must complete before HR records close; Finance final payroll depends on HR confirmation; if Finance fails, HR records must be reopened across an already-closed CS session. Options to evaluate: . | Classify intents at the gateway as вҖңindependent multi-domainвҖқ (current pattern) vs. вҖңdependent multi-domainвҖқ (needs orchestration) | Power Automate workflow as orchestrator for dependent processes вҖ” no session limits, native compensation branches, PA approval flows for human gates | Foundry Workflow (state machine) for compliance-grade processes requiring CMK or full audit trail | Define a Workflow Registry in Dataverse (parallel to Agent Registry): named workflows with ordered steps, compensating actions, timeout policies, and correlationId prefix per workflow | . DC-3 В· Indirect Prompt Injection . Gap: Content safety is applied at user input and agent output, but not specifically at tool response ingestion. At 100 agents making tool calls against enterprise systems (SharePoint, HRIS, CRM, Jira), a malicious document or record can embed adversarial instructions that hijack the agentвҖҷs next action вҖ” without triggering output-layer content safety. Options to evaluate: . | Add a response sanitisation step between tool response and LLM context re-entry вҖ” distinct from output content safety | Add to the Agent Design Template tool checklist: вҖңIs the tool response from a user-writable system? If yes, sanitisation required.вҖқ | Evaluate Azure Content Safety prompt shield for indirect injection detection on tool responses | . DC-4 В· Preview Dependency on Foundry Memory Feature . Gap: Cross-session user profile storage for Foundry agents is built on Memory Feature (preview). A 100-agent factory deployment targeting 6вҖ“12 months ships before GA is confirmed. API or behaviour changes in preview would require rebuilding the user profile layer across all Foundry agents. Options to evaluate: . | Build with Redis + Cosmos DB as the durable profile store now; add Memory Feature as a retrieval layer when GA | Gate Foundry agents on Memory Feature only after it reaches GA or a committed preview SLA is confirmed | Define a profile store abstraction so the underlying store can be swapped without rewriting agent logic | . DC-5 В· Model Selection Locked in Copilot Studio . Gap: Architectural Principle 16 recommends right-sizing the model per agent role (smaller models for classification, extraction, formatting). CS вҖ” recommended for 80вҖ“90% of agents вҖ” locks to Microsoft-managed models with no selection control. The principle is correct; the platform does not support it for the majority of agents. Options to evaluate: . | Explicitly document this as a CS tradeoff in the platform selection section | Route classification and extraction agents that would benefit from smaller models to Foundry instead of CS | Monitor whether CS adds model selection capability; treat Principle 16 as Foundry-only for now | . DC-6 В· LLM-as-Judge Calibration . Gap: LLM-as-Judge is recommended as the evaluation gate for routing accuracy and response quality. Known biases (positional, verbosity, self-enhancement) mean the judge can produce systematically wrong quality signals вҖ” giving false confidence before production promotion вҖ” unless calibrated against human annotations first. Options to evaluate: . | Establish human annotation agreement threshold (e.g., >80% agreement) as a prerequisite before LLM-as-Judge is used as an automated gate | Calibrate the judge during the Month 3вҖ“4 pilot wave, not after Month 5 scale-out begins | Use ensemble judging (multiple model judges + human spot-check) for high-stakes routing decisions | . DC-7 В· No Circuit Breaker or Tool Degradation Pattern . Gap: A 90% tool accuracy threshold exists as a deployment gate but there is no defined behaviour for tools that degrade below threshold after they are in production. A failing tool will continue to be called, accumulating errors and potentially corrupting downstream state across multiple agents. Options to evaluate: . | Define a degradation mode per agent: fallback to read-only / FAQ-only if agentic tools fail above a configurable error rate threshold | Add error rate monitoring in App Insights per tool per agent with an alert threshold and automatic tool disablement trigger | Leverage Principle 20 (kill switch) explicitly: document the per-tool disablement procedure as part of every Agent Design Template entry | . DC-8 В· Error Propagation Through the Agent Hierarchy . Gap: In a 4-hop chain (Gateway вҶ’ Domain Agent вҶ’ Specialist Agent вҶ’ Tool), there is no defined contract for how a tool failure propagates back to the user with enough context to be meaningful. Intermediate agents typically either swallow the error (wrong) or re-interpret it (introduces hallucination risk). Options to evaluate: . | Define a typed error envelope passed upstream untouched: { errorCode, errorMessage, retryable, agentId, stepId, correlationId } | Only the gateway translates the error envelope into user-facing language вҖ” intermediate agents forward, never interpret | Add error envelope to the Agent Design Template I/O contract section as a mandatory output field | . DC-9 В· correlationId Enforcement . Gap: The correlationId pattern ({domainCode}-{agentId}-{sessionTimestamp}) is a naming convention, not an enforced contract. In heterogeneous systems (CS + Foundry + Agentforce), propagation breaks silently вҖ” there is no validation step that rejects responses missing the expected correlationId. Options to evaluate: . | Add gateway-level validation: any agent response without a matching correlationId is treated as an error, not passed to the user | Define correlationId as a required field in the A2A payload schema, not an optional header | Add correlationId propagation test to the Agent Design Template Definition of Done checklist (already partially there вҖ” make it a gate, not a checkbox) | . Last reviewed: March 2026 В· Owner: Vineet Kaul .

8.doc: 2. How a User Instruction is Processed
8.title: 2. рҹ”„ How a User Instruction is Processed
8.content: рҹ‘Ө User: "Apply for leave and update my Salesforce records" в”Ӯ в–ј рҹҡӘ Gateway Agent (Copilot Studio) в”Ӯ FLOW: Classifies intent вҶ’ multi-domain в”Ӯ Disambiguation check: if intent is ambiguous вҶ’ enter в”Ӯ clarification topic BEFORE routing (HuggingFace finding: в”Ӯ ambiguity causes 50-point accuracy drop) в”Ӯ correlationId generated and propagated downstream в”Ӯ в–ј рҹ“Ұ HR Domain Agent (Copilot Studio) в”Ӯ FLOW: Routes to Leave Management child agent в”Ӯ Passes typed parameters (userId, leaveType) в”Ӯ в–ј рҹ”§ Leave Management Agent (CS connected child) в”Ӯ TOOL USE: PA flow вҶ’ reads HRIS (leave balance) в”Ӯ TOOL USE: PA flow вҶ’ submits leave request в”Ӯ VERIFICATION GATE: reads back confirmation state в”Ӯ deterministically вҖ” does NOT ask agent to confirm в”Ӯ its own output (FM-3.3: hallucinated success) в”Ӯ Returns: typed output (confirmationRef, managerEmail) в”Ӯ в–ј рҹ“Ұ HR Domain Agent aggregates вҶ’ passes result to Gateway в”Ӯ в–ј рҹҡӘ Gateway Agent в”Ӯ Routes CRM update to Agentforce (A2A + correlationId) в”Ӯ в–ј рҹҹҘ Agentforce Agent в”Ӯ Receives: A2A payload with full chat history + contextId в”Ӯ TOOL USE: Updates Salesforce record в”Ӯ Returns: CRM confirmation в”Ӯ в–ј рҹҡӘ Gateway aggregates both results вҶ’ composes final response в”Ӯ в–ј рҹ‘Ө "Leave approved (Ref: L2026-441). Salesforce updated." . рҹ“Ӣ Key Safeguards in the Flow . | Risk | Safeguard Applied | . | Agent verifies its own output (FM-3.3) | Deterministic read-back after every write action | . | Ambiguous user input causes wrong routing | Explicit disambiguation topic before any agentic branch | . | Agent loops without progress (FM-1.3) | Max step count + loop detector in Foundry orchestration code | . | Agent does not know when to stop (FM-1.5) | Termination conditions in code вҖ” never in LLM prompt | . | Tool argument malformed (>50% of failures per HuggingFace) | Structured error responses with format examples on every connector | . рҹ“ҡ References . | Copilot Studio вҖ” Multi-Agent Patterns | AssetOpsBench вҖ” Enterprise Agent Benchmarking | OpenEnv вҖ” Tool-Using Agents Evaluation | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” Tool Use Pattern | A2A Protocol | . рҹҸҶ Recommendation . вҡЎрҹ”ө This flow requires zero custom code. CS Topics handle routing, Power Automate handles tool calls, A2A/REST handles Agentforce. Build this in 1вҖ“2 sprints per domain. Do not add reflection or planning until the basic deterministic flow is stable and evaluated.

10.content: рҹҹҰ Copilot Studio as Parent . | вң… Pros | вқҢ Cons | . | В· No-code authoring вҖ” teams own independently | В· Sequential orchestration вҖ” child agents called one-at-a-time | . | В· Native M365 channels вҖ” Teams, SharePoint, M365 Copilot zero-setup | В· Session limits вҖ” 30 min idle, 60 min total, 100 turns | . | В· Shared container for CS children вҖ” variables, auth, Dataverse natively shared | В· Instructions size limit вҖ” hard cap of 8,000 characters | . | В· 1,400+ connectors out of the box | В· Model selection limited вҖ” locked to Microsoft-managed models | . | В· Agent templates + Component Collections вҖ” repeatable factory model | В  | . | В· Power Platform Pipelines вҖ” built-in DevвҶ’TestвҶ’Prod ALM | В  | . | В· Fastest time-to-first-agent across all platforms | В  | . | В· Agent 365 + CoE Toolkit governed natively in M365 Admin Center | В  | . рҹҹ§ Foundry Agent Service as Parent . | вң… Pros | вқҢ Cons | . | В· Parallel agent invocation via Foundry Workflows вҖ” fan-out/fan-in | В· Slower time-to-agent vs CS even with low-code tools | . | В· Foundry Flows (low-code) вҖ” visual drag-and-drop workflow designer | В· Foundry Connected Agents: max depth = 2 (hard limit) | . | В· OOB Foundry connectors to Azure, M365, and partner services | В· Session management вҖ” threadId registry pattern required | . | В· Deterministic Workflows вҖ” state machines for regulated processes | В· PaaS licensing cost on top of token costs | . | В· Native Memory (preview) вҖ” automatic cross-session user profiles | В· Foundry вҶ’ CS child integration still in preview | . | В· BYO Cosmos DB for data sovereignty (Standard setup only) | В  | . | В· Full OpenTelemetry distributed tracing | В  | . | В· Code Interpreter + File Search built in | В  | . рҹ’Ў Foundry is low-code too. Foundry Agent Service includes a visual Flows designer, pre-built connectors, and a portal UI for agent creation. The distinction from Copilot Studio is target audience and depth of control, not code vs. no-code. рҹ“ҡ References . | Microsoft вҖ” Copilot Studio vs Foundry | Microsoft CAF вҖ” Platform Selection | Foundry вҖ” Flows and Workflows | Microsoft Inside Track вҖ” CS or Foundry | . рҹҸҶ Recommendation . | Criterion | Verdict | . | вҡЎ Speed | Copilot Studio wins вҖ” hours to first agent vs. days | . | рҹ§© No-code | Copilot Studio wins вҖ” no-code native | . | рҹ“Ҳ Scale | Copilot Studio wins вҖ” agent templates, Component Collections, PP Pipelines | . | рҹҸӯ 100+ agents | Copilot Studio wins вҖ” factory model built for this scale | . | рҹ”ө Reduced complexity | Copilot Studio wins вҖ” single platform, Dataverse only, no extra infra | . Use Copilot Studio as the parent/master agent. Introduce Foundry as parent only for domains requiring parallel execution, deterministic state-machine workflows, or compliance-grade BYO storage. Target fewer than 3 Foundry-parent domains out of your total agent estate.

Use CS inline topics for tightly scoped sub-tasks within the same domain where shared context and zero-latency matter. Use CS connected agents when the sub-domain needs independent governance, team ownership, or reuse across multiple parents. Foundry agents are always in the connected tier (A2A) вҖ” never inline. Non-MSFT agents (Agentforce, ServiceNow) are connected tier only. Target ratio: 60% CS inline child, 25% CS connected, 10% Foundry connected, 5% non-MSFT connected.

14.title: 5. рҹ’ҫ Session Management, Transcript Storage & Persistent Storage
14.content: рҹ”„ Session Management . | Platform | How Session is Natively Managed | Mitigation | . | Copilot Studio | Automatic вҖ” session variables maintained per conversation; 30 min idle В· 60 min total В· 100 turns hard limits | Inactivity reset topic В· Store key state to session variables В· Async pattern for tasks >5 min | . | Foundry | Automatic вҖ” thread-based; each session is a thread (up to 100k messages); no time limit; threadId maps to userId | Redis for userIdвҶ’threadId registry (sub-ms lookup) В· Set own retention policy В· Use Memory feature for cross-session continuity | . | Agentforce | Salesforce-managed вҖ” session state owned by Salesforce platform | Pass correlationId in every A2A call to maintain continuity across platforms | . рҹ§  Context Architecture . HuggingFace / Forge RL research finding: attention dilution degrades performance as context grows. Implement explicit compaction: . | Tier | Name | What It Holds | CS Implementation | Foundry Implementation | Trigger | . | Tier 1 | Active Context | Current task В· recent tool results В· active variables | Managed automatically | Managed automatically | вҖ” | . | вҶ“ | Compaction step | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | N turns or token threshold reached | . | Tier 2 | Session Store | Intermediate results В· extracted entities В· task state | Dataverse table written via PA flow | Redis (hot session state, userIdвҶ’threadId) + Memory feature (preview) | Compaction step fires | . | вҶ“ | Archive step | вҖ” | вҖ” | вҖ” | Session close | . | Tier 3 | Long-Term Storage | User preferences В· past conversation summaries В· learned facts | Dataverse custom table (read via PA flow on session start) | CS-heavy: Dataverse via PA flow В· Foundry-heavy: CosmosDB В· Preview acceptable: Foundry Memory Feature | Session close | . рҹ“қ Conversation Transcript Storage . | Platform | Where Stored | Default Retention | For Compliance >30 Days | . | Copilot Studio | Dataverse ConversationTranscript (auto) | 30 days | Synapse Link вҶ’ Azure Data Lake | . | Foundry | Cosmos DB (Standard) or Microsoft-managed (Basic) | No auto-expiry вҖ” you set it | TTL policy on Cosmos DB containers | . | Agentforce | Salesforce Event Monitor | Salesforce-managed | Export via MuleSoft вҶ’ Azure Data Lake | . Cross-agent transcript correlation: . | CS inline child вҶ’ single parent transcript (no correlation needed) | CS connected child вҶ’ separate transcript вҶ’ join by correlationId | Foundry child вҶ’ Foundry thread вҶ’ join by correlationId | Agentforce вҶ’ Salesforce Event Monitor вҶ’ export and join by correlationId | . Decision Matrix вҖ” Which Technology Serves Each Requirement . Products in scope: Redis (cache) В· CosmosDB (session) В· SQL Server (profile) В· Dataverse В· Foundry Memory Feature . рҹҹҰ Copilot Studio . | Requirement | Storage Recommendation | Why вҖ” cannot be met by any other product | . | рҹ“қ Full conversation transcript | Dataverse | вҖў Auto-written by CS runtime to ConversationTranscript table вҖ” zero setup, no custom codeвҖў Native 30-day retention; extend via Synapse Link вҶ’ Azure Data Lake for complianceвҖў Redis: volatile by default вҖ” not durable for compliance transcriptsвҖў CosmosDB: not in CS native stack; requires custom integrationвҖў SQL Server: not in CS data path; requires custom connectorвҖў Foundry Memory Feature: unavailable in CS context | . | рҹ‘Ө User profile across sessions | Dataverse | вҖў Custom Dataverse table read via PA flow on session start вҖ” native, no extra infraвҖў Fully integrated with CS security model and Entra IDвҖў Redis: no native CS connector; volatile without persistence configвҖў SQL Server: possible but adds custom connector complexity; Dataverse already provisionedвҖў CosmosDB: not natively reachable from CS without custom codeвҖў Foundry Memory Feature: not available in CS context | . | рҹ”Қ Agent step recording | Dataverse | вҖў Custom table written via PA flow captures decision points and tool call outcomesвҖў Same managed identity and ALM lifecycle as the agent вҖ” no extra infrastructureвҖў Redis: not a durable audit log; evicts data without persistence configвҖў SQL Server: not natively accessible from CS Power Automate flowsвҖў CosmosDB: no native CS integration pathвҖў Foundry Memory Feature: not applicable to CS | . | вҡЎ Low latency (< 50ms) | CS Native Session Variables | вҖў Within-session state is held in CS runtime memory вҖ” no external store needed, zero network hopвҖў None of the 5 products applies: all are external stores with network overheadвҖў Dataverse (200вҖ“500ms REST) is the closest listed option but exceeds the 50ms targetвҖў Redis, CosmosDB, SQL Server, Foundry Memory Feature: not in the CS native data path | . рҹҹ§ Foundry AI . | Requirement | Storage Recommendation | Why вҖ” cannot be met by any other product | . | рҹ“қ Full conversation transcript | CosmosDB | вҖў thread-message-store stores every message verbatimвҖў agent-entity-store captures every LLM input/output with full payload and timestampвҖў Redis: volatile вҖ” data lost on eviction without persistence config; not a durable transcript storeвҖў SQL Server: no native schema for threaded conversations; concurrent writes degrade performance at scaleвҖў Dataverse: 200вҖ“500ms REST overhead + service protection throttling вҖ” unsuitable for high-volume real-time writesвҖў Foundry Memory Feature: stores extracted summaries only; raw transcript is permanently lost | . | рҹ‘Ө User profile across sessions | Option 1: Foundry Memory Feature вҡ пёҸ PreviewOption 2: CosmosDB вҖ” Foundry-heavy architectureOption 3: Dataverse via PA flow вҖ” CS-heavy architecture | Option 1 вҖ” Foundry Memory Feature (when preview is acceptable)вҖў Purpose-built for cross-session knowledge вҖ” AI-driven auto-extraction, zero custom codeвҖў Hybrid search retrieval (semantic + keyword) for context-aware profile readsвҖў Zero infrastructure вҖ” portal toggle onlyвҖў вҡ пёҸ Preview вҖ” not GA; do not use as sole production dependencyOption 2 вҖ” CosmosDB (when most agents in the architecture are Foundry-based)вҖў CosmosDB is already in the stack for transcript + step recording вҖ” no new infrastructureвҖў Durable, single-digit ms point reads by userId вҖ” direct SDK, no intermediaryвҖў Structured profile schema вҖ” deterministic, no AI extraction needed for known enterprise fieldsвҖў GA вҖ” no preview riskOption 3 вҖ” Dataverse via PA flow (when most agents in the architecture are Copilot Studio-based)вҖў Dataverse is already the dominant store вҖ” no new infrastructureвҖў Profile read once at session start вҖ” 200вҖ“500ms acceptable for session init, not mid-turnвҖў Requires PA flow intermediary (Foundry вҶ’ PA вҶ’ Dataverse); total ~1вҖ“2s at session startвҖў вҡ пёҸ Validate Dataverse service protection limits at concurrent session scale | . | рҹ”Қ Agent step recording | CosmosDB | вҖў agent-entity-store natively captures every reasoning step, tool call, and model decision with full payload and timestampвҖў No custom schema required вҖ” Foundry writes this automatically in Standard setupвҖў Redis: not a durable audit log; data lost on eviction without persistence configвҖў SQL Server: high-volume concurrent agent writes strain relational engines at scaleвҖў Dataverse: service protection limits and 200вҖ“500ms write latency unsuitable for high-frequency step loggingвҖў Foundry Memory Feature: not designed for execution step capture; no equivalent audit structure | . | вҡЎ Low latency (< 50ms) | Redis | вҖў Sub-millisecond key-value lookups вҖ” userIdвҶ’threadId mapping resolved per requestвҖў Hot user profile cache for prompt injection within a turnвҖў Scales horizontally with Azure Cache for RedisвҖў CosmosDB: single-digit ms вҖ” adequate for reads but not sub-ms for high-frequency per-turn lookupsвҖў SQL Server: 10вҖ“100ms, degrades significantly under concurrent agent loadвҖў Dataverse: 200вҖ“500ms REST overhead вҖ” fails the 50ms requirementвҖў Foundry Memory Feature: async extraction вҖ” not designed for real-time per-turn lookups | . рҹҹҘ Agentforce . вҡ пёҸ Agentforce runs on SalesforceвҖҷs own infrastructure. None of the five products (Redis, CosmosDB, SQL Server, Dataverse, Foundry Memory Feature) apply natively within Agentforce. Recommendations below follow Salesforce best practice. Integration with the Microsoft stack is via A2A/REST with correlationId propagation. | Requirement | Storage Recommendation | Why вҖ” cannot be met by any other product | . | рҹ“қ Full conversation transcript | Salesforce Event Monitoring | вҖў Platform-native audit trail for all agent interactions вҖ” no custom setup requiredвҖў Per Salesforce best practice: Event Monitoring is the authoritative source for Agentforce conversation logsвҖў For long-term retention: export via MuleSoft вҶ’ Azure Data Lake; join Microsoft-side traces by correlationIdвҖў None of the 5 Microsoft products are in the Agentforce runtime data path | . | рҹ‘Ө User profile across sessions | Salesforce Data Cloud | вҖў Unified customer profile across all Salesforce products вҖ” purpose-built for cross-session identityвҖў Per Salesforce best practice: Data Cloud consolidates CRM, Marketing Cloud, and agent interaction data into a single profileвҖў Einstein AI features provide semantic retrieval over unified profiles nativelyвҖў Agentforce reads Data Cloud profiles without custom integrationвҖў None of the 5 Microsoft products are accessible within Agentforce without an external API call | . | рҹ”Қ Agent step recording | Salesforce Event Monitoring + Agentforce Action Logs | вҖў Event Monitoring captures all agent actions, tool invocations, and decisions with timestampsвҖў Per Salesforce best practice: combine Event Monitoring with Agentforce-native Action Logs for full execution auditвҖў For Microsoft-side correlation: export logs and join by correlationId in Azure Data LakeвҖў None of the 5 Microsoft products are in the Agentforce execution path | . | вҡЎ Low latency (< 50ms) | Salesforce Platform Cache | вҖў SalesforceвҖҷs native cache layer (org cache + session cache) вҖ” sub-100ms reads within the Salesforce runtimeвҖў Per Salesforce best practice: Platform Cache is the recommended store for hot session state within Agentforce flowsвҖў None of the 5 Microsoft products are accessible within the Salesforce runtime without a round-trip API call, which exceeds the 50ms target | . Can Dataverse Replace Any of These? . With low latency (< 50ms) as a hard requirement, Dataverse qualifies only where it fully meets the requirement: . | Technology | Replaceable by Dataverse? | Reason | . | рҹ—„пёҸ SQL Server | вқҢ No | Dataverse REST API latency exceeds 50ms target for agent-speed concurrent reads and writes. SQL Server outperforms Dataverse for concurrent agent workloads. | . | рҹ§  Memory Feature | вқҢ No | Dataverse can store user profiles but cannot replicate AI-driven auto-extraction, semantic consolidation, or hybrid search retrieval. | . | вҡЎ Redis | вқҢ No | Dataverse REST API (200вҖ“500ms) cannot match Redis sub-millisecond latency. | . | рҹҹ§ Cosmos DB | вқҢ No | Dataverse cannot handle Foundry raw thread message volume, agent-entity-store LLM audit structure, or the latency requirements for real-time thread reads/writes. | . вҡ пёҸ With low latency as a hard requirement, Dataverse does not fully qualify as a replacement for any of the four storage technologies in a Foundry agent scenario. Dataverse remains correct for non-latency-sensitive operations: agent registry metadata, governance configuration, and CS transcript storage (auto-written, not in the real-time agent response path). Recommended Storage Assignment . рҹ“қ Full conversation transcript Copilot Studio вҶ’ Dataverse (auto, zero setup вҖ” CS only) Foundry agents вҶ’ Cosmos DB (Standard setup) (no low-latency alternative for full raw thread + LLM audit trail) рҹ‘Ө User profile across sessions Architecture lean drives the choice вҖ” provision one store, not one per agent type: CS-heavy вҶ’ Dataverse (via PA flow on session start) Already the dominant store В· zero new infra Foundry-heavy вҶ’ CosmosDB (already in stack for transcript + steps) Direct SDK read В· single-digit ms В· GA Preview OK вҶ’ Foundry Memory Feature AI-driven extraction В· semantic retrieval В· zero infra рҹ”Қ Agent step recording All Foundry вҶ’ Cosmos DB agent-entity-store + App Insights (latency, failure metrics) вҡЎ Low latency session state userIdвҶ’threadId вҶ’ Redis (sub-ms lookups during active sessions) User profile вҶ’ Memory Feature retrieval OR Redis cache . Updated Infrastructure Matrix . CS-heavy architecture (most agents on Copilot Studio): вң… Dataverse вҶ’ Transcripts (auto) В· user profiles В· agent metadata вң… Redis вҶ’ NOT REQUIRED for CS-only вқҢ CosmosDB вҶ’ NOT REQUIRED вқҢ Foundry Memory вҶ’ NOT REQUIRED Foundry-heavy architecture (most agents on Foundry): вң… CosmosDB вҶ’ Transcripts В· agent step recording В· user profiles вң… Redis вҶ’ Low latency session state (userIdвҶ’threadId) вң… App Insights вҶ’ Observability and metrics вқҢ Foundry Memory вҶ’ NOT REQUIRED (CosmosDB covers profiles) Mixed / Preview acceptable: вң… Foundry Memory вҶ’ User profiles вҖ” AI-driven В· zero infra В· вҡ пёҸ Preview вң… CosmosDB вҶ’ Transcripts + agent steps (if Foundry Standard) вң… Redis вҶ’ Low latency session state вң… Dataverse вҶ’ CS transcripts (auto) В· governance metadata . рҹ“ҡ References . | Copilot Studio вҖ” Transcript Controls | Foundry вҖ” Threads, Runs, Messages | Foundry вҖ” Memory | Foundry Standard Agent Setup | Cosmos DB Foundry Integration | Introducing Memory in Foundry | Forge RL вҖ” Context Compaction | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” Agent Memory | mem-agent Persistent Memory Research | Dataverse Capacity for Agents | . рҹҸҶ Recommendation . User profile store follows architecture lean вҖ” provision one store, not one per agent type. CS-heavy: Dataverse is the only store needed (transcripts auto-written, profiles via PA flow, session state in variables). Foundry-heavy: CosmosDB covers transcripts, agent steps, and profiles in one store; add Redis for low-latency session state. Preview acceptable: Foundry Memory Feature replaces CosmosDB for profiles with zero infrastructure. Do not mix profile stores across agent types вҖ” pick based on where your architecture leans and stay consistent.

Infrastructure follows architecture lean вҖ” provision for your dominant platform, not per agent type. CS-heavy: Dataverse only вҖ” no Redis, no Cosmos DB, no Foundry Memory Feature. 20.title: 8. рҹ“Ң Final Summary
20.content: вң… What to Build With . | Layer | Technology | Notes | . | рҹҡӘ Gateway + Domain Agents | Copilot Studio | No-code, fastest time to agent | . | рҹ”§ Specialist child agents | Copilot Studio (inline or connected) | CS domain agents only В· shared container, typed I/O | . | рҹ”— Specialist connected agents | Foundry Agent Service (A2A) | Complex reasoning, code execution, large RAG вҖ” connected tier only | . | рҹ”— Non-MSFT connected agents | Agentforce / ServiceNow (A2A/REST) | Connected tier only вҖ” never child agents | . | вҡЎ Workflows | Power Automate + Foundry Flows | No-code, 1,400+ connectors | . | рҹ”Ң Shared tools | MCP servers + Azure APIM | One integration per enterprise system | . | рҹ’ҫ CS agent data | Dataverse | Transcripts (auto), metadata, admin config | . | вҡЎ Foundry session state | Redis | Low latency userIdвҶ’threadId, hot cache | . | рҹ‘Ө Foundry cross-session memory | Memory Feature (preview) | User profiles, session summaries | . | рҹ—„пёҸ Foundry full transcript + audit | Cosmos DB (compliance only) | CMK / data residency / LLM audit trail | . | рҹ”Қ Large-scale RAG | Azure AI Search | Knowledge base > 500 docs | . | рҹ“Ғ Long-term archive | Azure Data Lake (Synapse Link) | Compliance retention > 30 days | . | рҹӣЎпёҸ Governance | Agent 365 + CoE Toolkit + Entra Agent ID | No custom audit infra needed | . вқҢ What to Avoid . | вқҢ Avoid | Why | . | Single orchestrator for all agents (star topology) | Unusable at 30+ agents, catastrophic at 100 | . | Cosmos DB for CS agents | Dataverse is the correct store; Cosmos DB is Foundry Standard only | . | SQL Server | No role вҖ” Dataverse covers admin data; Cosmos DB covers Foundry agent data | . | Redis for CS-only architecture | Not needed when Dataverse covers session state adequately | . | Agent Framework for CS agents | Dev complexity where no-code suffices | . | Free multi-agent peer-to-peer chaining | Least reliable pattern; orchestrator-controlled delegation only | . | Planning for known deterministic workflows | Use CS Topics / Foundry Workflows instead | . | Agent self-verification of its own output | FM-3.3: agents hallucinate success states вҖ” always use external verification | . | Building before defining termination conditions | FM-1.5: agents that do not know when to stop are the #1 failure predictor | . | Promoting tools below 90% accuracy | HuggingFace: 33-point accuracy gap; tool quality is the biggest performance lever | . рҹ—“пёҸ 100-Agent Delivery Timeline . Month 1вҖ“2: Platform foundation в”ңв”Җв”Җ Dev / Test / Prod environments (per domain) в”ңв”Җв”Җ ALM pipelines (Power Platform Pipelines) в”ңв”Җв”Җ 3вҖ“5 agent templates в”Ӯ Gateway В· Domain Agent В· FAQ Specialist в”Ӯ Task Specialist В· Integration Specialist в”ңв”Җв”Җ CoE Toolkit deployment в”ңв”Җв”Җ Entra Agent ID strategy в””в”Җв”Җ Agent 365 Frontier enrollment Month 3вҖ“4: Pilot wave (10вҖ“15 agents) в”ңв”Җв”Җ Gateway + 2вҖ“3 domain agents в”ңв”Җв”Җ Validate architecture, verification gates, в”Ӯ correlationId pattern, termination logic в”ңв”Җв”Җ Establish LLM-as-a-Judge evaluation baseline в””в”Җв”Җ Validate Agent 365 telemetry Month 5вҖ“12: Factory mode (8вҖ“12 agents/month) в”ңв”Җв”Җ Domain teams clone templates and configure в”ңв”Җв”Җ CoE reviews each agent before Test promotion в”ңв”Җв”Җ ALM pipeline promotes Test вҶ’ Prod automatically в””в”Җв”Җ Agent 365 registry grows with each release . Last updated: March 2026 Sources: Microsoft Cloud Adoption Framework В· Azure Architecture Center В· DeepLearning.AI Agentic Design Patterns В· HuggingFace / MAST / smolagents / AssetOpsBench / mem-agent В· Gartner Multiagent Systems Research В· Microsoft Agent Factory Blog Series .

22.title: 9. рҹ“Ӣ Agent Design Template
22.content: Complete one card per agent before building. This is the factory input document. рҹӘӘ 1. Identity . | Field | Value | . | Agent Name | e.g., HR Leave Management Agent | . | Agent ID | Unique ID вҖ” used as correlationId prefix | . | Version | e.g., 1.0.0 | . | Owner / Sponsor | Named individual accountable for this agent | . | Domain | e.g., Human Resources | . | Business Justification | What problem does this agent solve? What workflow does it replace? | . рҹ§© 2. Agent Classification . | Field | Options | Selected | . | Agent Type | Gateway В· Domain Agent В· Specialist Child В· Specialist Connected В· Inline Topic | В  | . | Platform | рҹҹҰ Copilot Studio В· рҹҹ§ Foundry Agent Service | В  | . | Agentic Pattern | рҹ”ө Flow (deterministic) В· рҹҹ  Crew (autonomous) | В  | . | Deployment Tier | Inline (shared with parent) В· Connected (independent) В· External (A2A) | В  | . вҡ пёҸ Start with Flow. Choose Crew only if the task is genuinely open-ended, dynamic adaptation is required, and errors are recoverable. рҹҺҜ 3. Scope and Responsibilities . Single Responsibility Statement: "This agent is responsible for _________________________________ and nothing else." In Scope: вҖў вҖў Out of Scope (explicitly): вҖў вҖў Orchestrator routing description (used by parent for routing): "Call this agent when the user wants to ________________________" (Write this carefully вҖ” it IS the routing signal) Ambiguity handling: What inputs require clarification before the agent acts? вҖў . рҹ“Ҙ 4. Input / Output Contract . Inputs . | Parameter | Type | Required? | Format / Example | . | e.g., userId | String | вң… Yes | AAD object ID | . | e.g., leaveType | Enum | вң… Yes | Annual | Sick | Unpaid | . | e.g., startDate | Date | вң… Yes | RFC3339: 2026-03-10 | . Outputs . | Parameter | Type | Always Returned? | Description | . | e.g., confirmationRef | String | вң… Yes | Leave request reference | . | e.g., status | Enum | вң… Yes | Approved | Pending | Rejected | . | e.g., errorCode | String | On failure only | Structured error for parent to handle | . рҹ”Ң 5. Tools and Integrations . | Tool Name | Type | System | Operation | Auth | Structured Error Response? | . | e.g., GetLeaveBalance | Power Automate | HRIS | Read | Managed Identity | вң… Yes | . | e.g., SubmitLeaveRequest | Power Automate | HRIS | Write | Managed Identity | вң… Yes | . Tool quality checklist (HuggingFace: >50% of agent failures from malformed tool args): . | Explicit argument formats with concrete examples in tool description | Datetime fields specify RFC3339 format with timezone offset | All error responses are structured and actionable (not silent failures or raw HTTP codes) | Tool tested independently before wiring into agent | Tool accuracy validated > 90% in test harness before production promotion | . Verification gates (MAST FM-3.3 вҖ” never self-verify): . | For every write operation: define the deterministic read-back step that confirms state change | Human-in-the-loop PA approval flow required before: ________ | . Side-channel state (Neuro SAN pattern): . | Sensitive values that must NOT appear in LLM prompt: ________ | Resolved at tool execution time via Key Vault reference: ________ | . рҹ“ҡ 6. Knowledge Sources . | Source | Type | Access | Size | If >500 docs вҶ’ Azure AI Search | . | e.g., HR Policy SharePoint site | SharePoint | Read | ~50 docs | No | . рҹ’ҫ 7. Session and Memory . | Concern | CS Approach | Foundry Approach | . | Within-session state | Topic/global variables | Thread messages (managed) | . | Context compaction | Summarisation PA flow at N turns | Memory feature + compaction step | . | Cross-session user profile | Dataverse custom table (PA flow reads on start) | Memory feature (preview) | . | Inactivity handling | Inactivity topic at __ minutes вҶ’ clear variables | Thread retained; new thread on next session | . | Long-running tasks | Async: trigger PA вҶ’ notify via Teams card | Async: Durable Function вҶ’ push notification | . | Max session turns | Design to complete in < 80 turns | No limit but implement compaction at N turns | . рҹ”Ғ 8. Agentic Behaviour . | Pattern | Enabled? | Configuration | . | Tool Use | вң… Always | List tools in section 5; merge tools where possible | . | Clarification | вң… Always | Define ambiguous input conditions; enter clarification topic before any agentic branch | . | Termination Logic | вң… Always | Max steps: __ В· Loop detector: same tool + same args = break В· Success state: deterministic (not LLM-reasoned) | . | External Verification | вң… For writes | Read-back step after every state-changing tool call | . | Reflection | вҳҗ Optional | Trigger: quality-sensitive outputs (reports, code, plans). Critic prompt: вҖңReview for accuracy, completeness, and policy compliance.вҖқ | . | Planning | вҳҗ Crew only | Enable only if task is genuinely open-ended | . | Multi-agent delegation | вҳҗ Domain agents only | List connected specialist agents with routing descriptions | . рҹӣЎпёҸ 9. Security and Governance . | Field | Value | . | Entra Agent Identity | Agent ID registered in Entra: ______ | . | Credential Type | Managed Identity (preferred) В· Federated В· OAuth2 | . | Permitted Tool Scopes | List APIs/connectors this agent is allowed to call | . | Prohibited Scopes | What this agent must NOT have access to | . | Data Classification | Public В· Internal В· Confidential В· Restricted | . | PII Handled? | Yes / No вҶ’ If Yes: Foundry Standard + CMK required | . | Sensitive state in side channel? | Yes / No вҶ’ If Yes: Key Vault references, never in prompt | . | Content Safety | Input filter: вң… / Output filter: вң… | . рҹ”— 10. Connected Agents (Domain Agents Only) . | Specialist Agent | Platform | Routing Description | Calls Per Session | . | e.g., Leave Management Agent | CS | вҖңCall when user requests leave application or balanceвҖқ | 1 | . | e.g., HR Policy Agent | CS | вҖңCall when user asks about HR policies or entitlementsвҖқ | 3 | . вҡ пёҸ Do not exceed 8вҖ“10 specialist agents per domain agent. Keep total tools + agents bounded вҖ” routing accuracy degrades as scope grows. рҹ“Ҡ 11. Observability and Evaluation . | Metric | Target | Measurement | . | Task completion rate | > 90% | CS Analytics / Foundry traces | . | Tool call accuracy | > 90% | Test harness pre-production (HuggingFace deployment gate) | . | Routing accuracy | > 95% | LLM-as-a-Judge on sample sessions | . | Average turns to completion | < 5 | CS Analytics | . | Session escalation rate | < 10% | CS Analytics | . | Loop detection events | 0 in production | Foundry orchestration logs | . | FM-3.3 (self-verification) events | 0 | Verification gate audit log | . Evaluation approach: . | Deterministic checks for structured outputs | LLM-as-a-Judge for response quality | Human annotation sample (monthly calibration) | . correlationId pattern: {domainCode}-{agentId}-{sessionTimestamp} . рҹҸ—пёҸ 12. ALM and Deployment . | Field | Value | . | Solution Name | e.g., HR.LeaveManagement.Agent | . | Publisher Prefix | e.g., contoso | . | Base Template Used | e.g., Specialist-Task-Agent-Template-v1 | . | Dev Environment | В  | . | Test Environment | В  | . | Prod Environment | В  | . | ALM Pipeline | Power Platform Pipelines В· GitHub Actions В· Azure DevOps | . | Environment Variables | All config values externalised (endpoints, IDs, feature flags) | . | Dependencies | Parent agents, shared PA flows, MCP servers, Dataverse tables | . вң… 13. Definition of Done . | Single responsibility statement is clear and agreed with business owner | All ambiguous input conditions have a clarification topic | Input/output contract defined and tested | All tool calls tested independently at > 90% accuracy | Structured error responses on every tool connector | External verification gate built for every write operation | Termination conditions defined in code (max steps, loop detector) | Sensitive state moved to side channel (Key Vault) вҖ” not in prompt | Inactivity/compaction topic built (CS agents) | Entra Agent Identity created, scoped with least privilege, sponsor assigned | correlationId propagation tested end-to-end | Deployed through ALM pipeline (never manual publish) | Registered in Agent 365 / CoE Toolkit inventory | . рҹ“ҡ References . | Copilot Studio вҖ” ALM Strategy | Copilot Studio вҖ” Agent Templates | Microsoft Entra Agent ID | DeepLearning.AI вҖ” Evaluating AI Agents | HuggingFace вҖ” Agent Leaderboard | Power Platform вҖ” Component Collections | .
